title: Employment Opportunities at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace
gist: This is a Carnegie Endowment careers/recruitment landing page with no analytical or market content; it contains only organizational boilerplate about employment, fellowships, internships, and diversity.

insights.claim: The Carnegie Endowment for International Peace describes itself as a community of more than 300 people across twenty countries providing policymakers with independent insights on international peace.
insights.speaker: Carnegie Endowment
insights.so_what: Context only on the source’s scale; no actionable market signal.
insights.locator: The Carnegie Endowment is a community of more than 300 people

insights.claim: The Carnegie Endowment runs the James C. Gaither Junior Fellows Program, offering roughly fifteen one-year fellowships to recent graduating seniors who work as research assistants to senior scholars.
insights.speaker: Carnegie Endowment
entities: James C. Gaither Junior Fellows Program
insights.so_what: Recruiting-program detail only; no investment relevance.
insights.locator: James C. Gaither Junior Fellows Program

top_entities: James C. Gaither Junior Fellows Program
